section: Class X – English – First Flight – Poem – Amanda

activities.title: Understanding the Speaker
activities.objective: Identify who is speaking in the poem and whom they are speaking to.
activities.steps: 1. Read the poem 'Amanda'. 2. Identify the lines spoken by the parent. 3. Identify Amanda’s imagined thoughts written in brackets. 4. Write who is speaking in each part.
activities.learning_outcome: Students can distinguish between the parent’s voice and Amanda’s imagination.
activities.evaluation: Correct identification of speakers in the poem.

activities.title: Amanda’s Imaginary World
activities.steps: 1. Read the stanzas in brackets carefully. 2. List the three imaginary characters Amanda imagines herself as. 3. Write one line about why she wants to be each character.
activities.learning_outcome: Students understand how imagination reflects Amanda’s desire for freedom.
activities.evaluation: Ability to identify and explain Amanda’s imagined roles.

activities.title: Theme of Freedom
activities.objective: Recognize the theme of freedom in the poem.
activities.steps: 1. Read the poem again. 2. Find lines that show Amanda wants freedom. 3. Write 4–5 sentences explaining why she feels restricted.
activities.learning_outcome: Students understand the main theme of freedom and control.

activities.title: Identify Repetition
activities.objective: Notice the repeated use of Amanda’s name.
activities.steps: 1. Count how many times 'Amanda' is repeated in the poem. 2. Discuss why the poet repeats her name. 3. Write your answer in 3–4 sentences.
activities.learning_outcome: Students understand the effect of repetition in poetry.

activities.title: Character Sketch
activities.steps: 1. Read the poem carefully. 2. List words that describe Amanda (e.g., imaginative, sensitive). 3. Write a short character sketch in 5–6 sentences.
activities.learning_outcome: Students can describe a character based on textual clues.
activities.evaluation: Relevance and clarity of character description.

activities.title: Role Play Activity
activities.steps: 1. One student acts as Amanda. 2. Another student acts as the parent. 3. Read the lines with proper expression. 4. Discuss how Amanda feels.
activities.learning_outcome: Students develop empathy and expressive reading skills.

activities.title: Vocabulary Builder
activities.objective: Learn meanings of new words from the poem.
activities.steps: 1. Find 5 new words (e.g., languid, tranquil, sulking). 2. Write their meanings. 3. Use each word in a sentence.

activities.title: Message of the Poem
activities.steps: 1. Think about what the poet wants to say about parents and children. 2. Write a short paragraph (5–6 lines) about the message of the poem.
activities.learning_outcome: Students understand the central idea of the poem.
activities.evaluation: Clarity and relevance of the written paragraph.

activities.title: Drawing Amanda’s Dream
activities.steps: 1. Choose one imaginary scene (mermaid, orphan, Rapunzel). 2. Draw the scene. 3. Write 2 lines describing your drawing.
activities.learning_outcome: Students connect imagination with visual expression.
activities.evaluation: Creativity and connection with the poem.

activities.title: Short Summary Writing
activities.objective: Summarize the poem in simple words.
activities.steps: 1. Read the poem carefully. 2. Write the main idea in 5–6 simple sentences. 3. Check if you included Amanda’s feelings and the parent’s behavior.
activities.learning_outcome: Students practice summarizing and understanding key ideas.
